steps.title: Find the decay factor
steps.content: The sample loses 24% of its mass each 22.96‑minute interval, so the fraction that remains is $$1-0.24 = 0.76$$.

steps.title: Write the exponential decay model
steps.content: The exponential decay model is $$M = M_0 \cdot r^{\frac{t}{T}}$$, where $$M_0$$ is the initial mass, $$r$$ is the remaining fraction per period, and $$T$$ is the length of one period.

steps.title: Match to answer choices
steps.content:
Substituting $$M_0 = 100$$ grams, $$r = 0.76$$, and $$T = 22.96$$ minutes gives $$M = 100(0.76)^{\frac{t}{22.96}}$$. This expression matches choice D.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
